Acceptance Handoff · Source of truth

ML Models

Train, evaluate, and version page classifiers inside the FlowX Designer — a frontend design prototype, fully mocked in memory.

Accepted 2 Jul 2026 PR #6212 · View pull request ↗

Prototype goal

Teams building document-processing workflows need to classify document pages into categories (national ID, passport, utility bill, payslip…), but there is no in-Designer way to define, train, evaluate, and version a page classifier.

This prototype explores a self-contained ML Models surface where a designer defines the classes, labels a corpus of pages, trains immutable model “candidates”, evaluates them on a fixed held-out benchmark, and chooses which version runs in workflows — without leaving the Designer or touching a data-science pipeline.

Summary

Adds a new ML Models area under Integrations (between Data Sources and Workflows). A list page creates and browses models; a fullscreen detail page carries three tabs:

Trained Candidates — a roster of immutable trained versions with one “main” pointer, promote-to-main (with confirm), and a two-candidate compare.  Data — add labeled pages via a keyboard-first mixed-batch auto-label review or a per-class upload, plus a corpus table that doubles as the training-selection surface.  Classes — the categories the model sorts pages into, each with positive/negative example snippets, plus a locked UNKNOWN default.

Two fullscreen sub-routes: a per-candidate details page (clickable heat-mapped confusion matrix, training distribution, held-out testing pool, example-improvement suggestions) and a two-candidate comparison page (how-it-tested delta, Δ confusion matrix, per-class distribution deltas, improved/degraded diff). All data is mocked in-memory and resets on refresh.

Where this applies

Screens
ML Models list · Model detail (Trained Candidates / Data / Classes) · Mixed-batch auto-label review · Candidate details · Candidate comparison · Empty/untrained model
Aligned to
The FlowX Design System — flx-resource-header sub-header (the same dark bar as a Data Source detail page), DS Tabs spec (underline, #006bd8 active), and DS form controls (flx-checkbox, flx-switch, segmented control) with DS color tokens rather than ad-hoc inline classes.
